This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim lead in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner at 18.8%, closely trailed by California Governor Gavin Newsom (16.7%) and Secretary of State Marco Rubio (15.4%), reflecting their prominent roles in the Trump administration and Democratic ranks during an nascent shadow primary. Recent Echelon Insights polling on April 21 highlighted shifting preferences among potential contenders, while Newsom headlined a high-profile Democratic governors' fundraiser in Los Angeles on April 24, elevating his national visibility. President Trump's repeated praise for both Vance and Rubio as "unstoppable" has fueled GOP speculation without clear separation. With 2026 midterms looming, strong performances in swing states or battlegrounds, formal campaign launches, or shifts in polling averages could widen gaps in this highly fluid field.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold June 2026 futures (GCM26) trade near $4,724 per ounce, embodying trader consensus on sustained central bank buying and sticky inflation dynamics despite the Federal Reserve's federal funds rate hold at 3.50%-3.75% through April 2026. Prices consolidated around $4,700-$4,760 in early May after a 25% year-to-date surge, propelled by emerging market diversification from U.S. dollar reserves and geopolitical risks. UBS projects $5,900/oz by late 2026, while J.P. Morgan eyes $5,000 in Q4, reflecting market-implied probabilities of higher-for-longer rates boosting gold's safe-haven bid. Key catalysts include mid-May CPI data and the June 11-12 FOMC meeting, with 10-year Treasury yields near 4.2% dictating real yield sensitivity.

markets.question: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF: O/U 9.5 Total Corners
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 10 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 10, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF - Total Corners
events.description: Total corners markets for the La Liga game between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona top the La Liga table with a commanding lead over second-placed Real Madrid ahead of their pivotal El Clásico on May 10 at Spotify Camp Nou, where high-stakes title implications fuel aggressive play. Both sides generate substantial corners—Barcelona averaging 7.35 at home with matches totaling 11.27, Real Madrid 6.13 away amid 10.18 overall—reflecting possession-dominant styles and frequent wide attacks under Hansi Flick and Carlo Ancelotti. Recent developments include Kylian Mbappé's hamstring injury rendering him doubtful, compounded by Rodrygo's season-ending ACL tear, potentially limiting Madrid's crossing volume, while Raphinha eases back from his own hamstring issue for Barça. Past Clásicos average over 11 corners, underscoring rivalry-driven intensity.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on peak 2026 inflation hinges on March data showing U.S. CPI accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—from 2.4% prior, propelled by a 12.5% energy price surge and tariff passthrough adding ~0.8 percentage points to core PCE at 3.2%. Headline PCE hit 3.5%, exceeding expectations and prompting the Federal Reserve's March Summary of Economic Projections to quietly lift its 2026 median forecast above prior 2.4%. Polymarket odds reflect this shift, with traders pricing elevated but contained risks below 4%, amid base effects and potential Fed rate adjustments. Key catalyst: April CPI release on May 12, alongside June FOMC, where persistent services inflation could sustain upside pressures or signal disinflation if goods cool.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in detention following his February 2026 life imprisonment sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his 2024 martial law declaration, after the Constitutional Court upheld his impeachment and removal from office in April 2025. Multiple convictions, including a five-year term for obstructing arrest (upheld and increased to seven years by an appeals court on April 29, 2026), reinforce trader consensus implying a 92.1% probability he stays in custody through 2026. Ongoing appeals in his insurrection trial, with hearings as recent as May 7, show no bail grants or release signals, amid procedural hurdles like extended detention warrants that historically prolong high-profile cases. Late-breaking acquittals or health-related pardons could shift odds, though none appear imminent.

eventMetadata.context_description: Geopolitical tensions surrounding Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz since late February have triggered the largest supply disruption in oil market history, removing up to 14 million barrels per day and propelling WTI crude front-month futures to around $95 per barrel as of May 10, amid sharp volatility from $110 peaks. The latest EIA data for the week ending May 1 revealed falling U.S. crude inventories and elevated refinery utilization, amplifying tightness despite OPEC+'s modest May production hike of 206,000 barrels per day. Traders are pricing in persistent risk premiums, with backwardation in the futures curve signaling near-term scarcity. Key catalysts ahead include the May 13 EIA report, potential U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases, and summer driving season demand peaking by June end, though high prices risk curbing consumption and easing pressures.

markets.question: Will Arsenal score the most goals in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/statistics/clubs/goals/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain holds a commanding 44 goals in 16 UEFA Champions League matches, recently overtaking Bayern Munich's 43 after a record-breaking semi-final tie totaling 11 goals across legs (PSG advancing 6-5 aggregate). With Bayern eliminated, Arsenal trails at 29 goals entering the final despite strong knockout form, including clean sheets in prior rounds. Trader consensus prices PSG at 99.4% implied probability to claim top-scoring club honors, reflecting the insurmountable 15-goal deficit—Arsenal would require scoring 16+ more than PSG in one match, an unprecedented outcome. PSG's distributed attack, led by Kvaratskhelia (10 goals), bolsters their position amid peaking momentum.

markets.question: Will Anthropic be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the tech sector hinges on intensifying AI consolidation, exemplified by SpaceX's April 21 announcement of a $60 billion option to acquire Cursor, the fast-growing AI coding assistant rivaling GitHub Copilot after Microsoft passed on a bid, amid surging demand for developer tools to accelerate model training. Caesars Entertainment faces active takeover talks with billionaire Tilman Fertitta for around $7 billion since March, reflecting casino sector M&A heat. Biotech standout Viking Therapeutics draws bids on its promising obesity drug pipeline, VK2735, amid pharma acquisition frenzy. Key watchpoints include SpaceX-Cursor regulatory reviews, GitLab and Zoom Q2 earnings in late May, and broader antitrust scrutiny on Big Tech deals like potential Perplexity AI plays, all poised to sway outcomes before year-end resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jeddah be 36°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sa/jeddah/OEJN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 35°C (23.5% implied probability) and 36°C (21.0%) for Jeddah's highest temperature on May 11, reflecting tight forecast model spreads amid a dominant subtropical high-pressure ridge over the Arabian Peninsula. Recent National Center for Meteorology (NCM) guidance and ECMWF/GFS ensembles project highs of 36-37°C, rebounding from 33°C observed on May 7 after thunderstorms and convective activity cooled surface temperatures. Key differentiators include Red Sea sea-breeze effects capping coastal peaks, potential residual haze or clouds delaying peak insolation, and urban heat at King Abdulaziz International Airport. Historical May averages near 37°C provide context, but current drier conditions favor slight moderation; watch NCM's daily update and tomorrow's model runs for shifts.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

The March dot plot median envisions gradual easing to around 3.1% by end-2027, contingent on sustained disinflation, contrasting with market-implied paths from CME FedWatch Tool showing high odds of no near-term change. Polymarket traders' skin-in-the-game consensus weighs these dynamics, with pivotal catalysts including April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC gathering.

markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 has solidified trader consensus at 95.5% implied probability for the best coding AI model by end of May, driven by its commanding leads across key benchmarks like SWE-bench Verified (over 90% resolution rates) and LMSYS Code Arena, where it outpaces rivals by 40+ points in agentic coding tasks such as building live web apps. Released in mid-April, the model excels in multi-file reasoning, bug fixing, and real-world software engineering, per official evaluations and third-party leaderboards, outshining OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro. While uncertainty persists—potential GPT-5.5 launches or Google I/O surprises could narrow the gap—Anthropic's multi-model dominance (multiple Claudes in top ranks) and lack of recent challenger breakthroughs underpin the strong positioning, with resolution hinging on late-May updates.

markets.question: Telstar 1963 vs. Heracles Almelo: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between Telstar 1963 and Heracles Almelo, scheduled for May 10 at 10:45 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Telstar 1963 and Heracles Almelo combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Telstar 1963 vs. Heracles Almelo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for May 10 at 10:45 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Telstar host Heracles Almelo in a pivotal Eredivisie relegation showdown at BUKO Stadion on May 10, with both clubs mired near the table bottom and desperate for points to avoid direct drop or playoffs. Telstar remain winless in their last six league games, struggling for goals despite modest home record of three wins, while Heracles endure a nine-match winless streak including seven straight away losses, exposing defensive frailties. Their season encounter ended 1-1, preserving Heracles' unbeaten streak across eight head-to-heads. Injuries sideline Telstar's Nökkvi Thorisson and Dion Malone alongside Heracles' Mike Te Wierik and Jeff Reine-Adélaide, heightening uncertainty in this low-scoring, survival clash.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's highly anticipated ninth studio album ICEMAN, officially slated for May 15 release via OVO Sound and Republic Records, drives trader sentiment through a masterful rollout capped by a viral Toronto ice block revealing the date on April 21. Fresh sightings of Drake filming an ICEMAN music video in the city over the weekend signal final promotional push amid cryptic Instagram quotes hinting at beef callouts and social media critiques. Polymarket consensus, backed by $59K volume, clusters around lyrical nods to fatherhood ("Daddy" frontrunner), Toronto sports icons like Kawhi Leonard, and cultural callbacks such as Covid, reflecting Drake's signature blend of personal disses, family lore, and regional pride from past projects. Resolution awaits official Spotify transcripts post-debut, with charts and streaming metrics to follow.

markets.question: Will Ben Griffin win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Entering the final round of the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, Alex Fitzpatrick holds a one-stroke lead at 14-under after a third-round 64, with Kristoffer Reitan (also 64) one back at 13-under and former Wake Forest teammate Cameron Young two strokes off the pace at 12-under following his sizzling 63 featuring an eagle and six birdies in nine holes. Trader consensus reflects this tight leaderboard bunching, as Reitan's late birdie run and Young's momentum offset Fitzpatrick's slim edge in his first 54-hole lead on TOUR, while Harry Hall lurks eight back in a tie for 13th. Quail Hollow's demanding length and greens keep the outcome fluid for this $20 million signature event, amplifying upset potential among the top contenders.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

The tournament features 11 teams—two seeds each from LCK, LPL, LEC, LCS, and LCP, plus one from CBLOL—via Spring Split playoffs using Fearless Draft Bo3/Bo5 formats. Hanwha Life Esports leads LCK at 10-1, Bilibili Gaming dominates LPL Split 2 at 9-2, Team Vitality tops LEC at 8-1, while Cloud9 and Team Liquid sit undefeated at 5-0 in LCS. Playoff brackets start soon, with roster health, meta adaptation, and head-to-head momentum poised to influence final qualifiers amid tight races.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Aubry Bracco as Survivor 50 frontrunner at 87% implied probability, propelled by her under-the-radar strategic prowess surviving the episode 11 double elimination that blindsided Ozzy Lusth—despite his unused idol—and ousted Emily Flippen. Aubry's push to exploit Ozzy's loose lips about his day-one Cirie Fields alliance showcased her social read and alliance flexibility within the Rick Devens-Emily trio remnant, positioning her ideally among the final seven alongside Jonathan Young (immunity winner), Joe Hunter, Rizo Velovic, Tiffany Ervin, Rick, and Cirie (7.2%). Her past runner-up finishes in Kaôh Rōng and Game Changers bolster jury management expectations, though Cirie's extra-vote orchestration keeps her viable. With the finale looming in two weeks, next immunity and blindside risks could solidify or shatter this momentum.

markets.question: Will Benoni Mendes win the 2026 Minas Gerais gubernatorial election?

eventMetadata.context_description: Senator Cleitinho Azevedo (Republicanos) commands trader consensus at 56.5% implied probability to win the Minas Gerais governorship on October 4, 2026, driven by consistent leads in recent polls, including the Doxa survey of May 8 showing him at 28-30% in first-round scenarios ahead of Alexandre Kalil (PDT) at 21-24%, with a narrow 37-34% runoff edge over Kalil. Earlier Quaest polling from late April reinforced his dominance across matchups, capturing strong right-wing support amid a fragmented field lacking incumbent Romeu Zema. Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) trails at 18.5% market odds despite slipping to 6% in the latest poll, while Gabriel Azevedo (MDB) at 13.6% and others lag, highlighting opposition consolidation risks ahead of the first-round vote.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Hakeem Jeffries' commanding position as House Minority Leader in the solidly Democratic NY-08 district, rated Solid D by Cook Political Report with a D+26 partisan lean, drives trader consensus heavily favoring the Democratic Party at 92% for the November 3 general election. Jeffries dominates the June 23 Democratic primary market at 96% against challenger Vance Bostic, reflecting strong fundraising and establishment support amid minimal recent primary turbulence. No prominent Republican nominee has emerged, underscoring historical lopsided margins exceeding 70%. Scenarios like a primary upset yielding a flawed nominee, Jeffries scandal, or national Republican wave could shift odds, though structural advantages make an upset unlikely.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vienna hosts Eurovision 2026 at Wiener Stadthalle with semi-finals on May 12 and 14, and the final on May 16, driving intense trader focus on Top 3 contenders amid completed national selections and all 35 songs released. Bookmaker frontrunners like Finland's violin-driven "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen lead after dominating OGAE fan polls and impressing in second rehearsals, while Denmark, Greece, France, and Australia show strong staging and televote potential. Recent comebacks by Romania and Bulgaria heighten competition, but semi-final qualification—hinging on live execution and jury-televote splits—remains the key catalyst, echoing past Nordic dominance in close races.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley at 47.5% implied probability as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated "Project Apex" IPO—a $75 billion raise at up to $1.75 trillion valuation targeting late June 2026—owing to its longstanding advisory role with Elon Musk, including Tesla's 2010 listing, and the February rehiring of key dealmaker Michael Grimes. Reuters reporting from early April confirms Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs (17.5%), Bank of America (15%), and JPMorgan among five active bookrunners in a 21-bank syndicate, fueling sentiment shifts. With the S-1 prospectus due late May and roadshow starting June 8, traders eye resolution details on the prized "lead left" spot amid massive retail allocation.

markets.question: Nvidia Data Center Revenue above 50B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nvidia’s data center revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2027, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.
The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Nvidia’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

Management's $78 billion (±2%) total revenue guidance—explicitly excluding China data center compute amid U.S. export curbs—implies sequential acceleration, with analysts projecting data center sales around $70 billion fueled by Blackwell GPU ramp-up and hyperscaler AI capex surges. Key watchpoints include gross margins (guided 74.9%) and Q2 outlook; earnings release on May 20 could trigger volatility based on guidance beats versus Street estimates.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no Trump-Putin in-person meeting by June 30 at 93.8%, driven by the absence of any announced summit plans amid ongoing Ukraine ceasefire talks following President Trump's May 8 declaration of a three-day pause and Putin's recent suggestion the war nears an end. Logistical hurdles, security protocols, and lack of diplomatic momentum since the leaders' last 2025 Alaska summit reinforce this positioning. markets.question: Will the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting be on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date (Pakistan Standard Time) on which the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran occurs.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: T1's competitive 6-3 record in the LCK 2026 Spring Split, placing them third behind dominant Hanwha Life Esports and KT Rolster, balances trader sentiment on roster stability versus adjustment needs, with Yes at 53% reflecting Doran's contract expiring at season's end amid the team's recovery from an early 3-3 slump. Long-term deals—Faker to 2029, Oner and Peyz to 2028, Keria to 2029—anchor the core, reinforced by recent staff tweaks like Easyhoon's coaching addition following kkOma's temporary leave. Prolonged struggles or a shallow playoff exit could spur mid-split benching or top-lane swap before July's Summer lock; conversely, a deep run would solidify the lineup per T1's history of international peaks despite domestic inconsistencies.

markets.question: Will Elina Svitolina win the 2026 Women’s US Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 U.S. Open tennis tournament is scheduled for August 23 - September 13, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after October 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. Open (https://www.usopen.org/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins Aryna Sabalenka atop the 2026 US Open women's field at 26% implied probability, buoyed by her sustained world No. 1 ranking and proven hard-court dominance, including deep Grand Slam runs, though her recent Rome round-of-32 exit tempers momentum. Elena Rybakina trails closely at 20% after surging past Swiatek in the Australian Open quarters and clinching Stuttgart—her second clay crown—highlighting her versatile baseline power ideal for Flushing Meadows. Iga Swiatek's 16% reflects clay mastery ahead of Roland Garros but spotty hard-court Slam results, while 19-year-old Victoria Mboko's breakout top-10 ascent (19-7 YTD) and WTA 2025 Revelation award fuel her 15% bid amid a parity-packed draw featuring Gauff's home-soil edge and no injury disruptions, keeping the race tightly contested through the clay-hard transition.

markets.question: Will Olympiacos win the 2025-26 Euroleague?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club wins the 2025-2026 Euroleague season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to win the 2025-2026 Euroleague per the rules of the Euroleague (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Euroleague (https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/); however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olympiacos tops trader consensus at 37% implied probability after sweeping AS Monaco 3-0 in quarterfinals, including a dominant 105-82 Game 3 clincher four days ago, securing their fifth straight Final Four berth as regular-season No. 1 seed with elite offensive rating. Fenerbahçe Beko follows at 25.5% following a 3-1 series lead over Zalgiris Kaunas capped by a 94-90 Game 4 road win, ending a late regular-season skid with strong defensive showings. Real Madrid sits at 19% despite center Edy Tavares' knee injury sidelining him for playoffs, advancing 3-1 versus Hapoel Tel Aviv via depth in an 87-81 Game 4 clincher. Panathinaikos holds 16.5% amid a resilient 2-2 quarterfinal tie with No. 2 Valencia Basket—losing Game 4 86-89 in Athens but eyeing Game 5 upset potential ahead of the home Final Four in Athens on May 22-24, despite Kostas Sloukas' early meniscus absence. Valencia trails at 8.6%, forcing Game 5 at home after stealing momentum.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's trader-implied 37% championship probability stems from his breakout dominance, clinching three straight Grand Prix victories—including pole-to-win at the Miami GP last weekend—extending his drivers' standings lead to 20 points (100-80) over Mercedes teammate George Russell, whose consistent P4 finish there highlighted the intra-team pressure. Mercedes' early constructors' lead underscores their superior pace amid Red Bull's struggles, keeping Russell at 29.5% as a credible threat with strong qualifying and race craft. McLaren's Norris (10.9%) and Piastri (5.4%) gained momentum via Miami podiums, while Verstappen's lowly 6.3% reflects Red Bull's form dip, fueling a tight top-heavy market with 18 rounds left for swings via reliability, strategy, or upgrades.

markets.question: Will Benoît Saint Denis fight Charles Oliveira next?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Dan Hooker (43.9% implied probability) as Charles Oliveira's next opponent following his dominant unanimous decision win over Max Holloway to claim the BMF title at UFC 326 in March, driven by Hooker's persistent callouts, claims of UFC interest in the striker vs. grappler matchup, and Oliveira's fiery post-fight dismissal accusing him of fixation. Arman Tsarukyan sits close at 34.9%, boosted by their prior back-and-forth war—likely a UFC 300 lightweight eliminator—and Tsarukyan's recent statements prioritizing a BMF rematch if no title shot materializes. Benoît Saint Denis (19.4%) gains from his ascent as a knockout artist in lightweight rankings amid the division's contender logjam, while Oliveira's new eight-fight UFC extension through age 40 underscores imminent activity without an official announcement.

markets.question: Will Broadcom be the second-largest company in the world by market cap on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the second-largest company in the world by market cap on May 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: 2nd largest company end of May?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the second-largest company in the world by market cap on May 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alphabet's $4.81 trillion market capitalization, cementing its current second-place ranking behind NVIDIA's $5.23 trillion, underpins the 87.5% implied probability from trader consensus that it will remain the second-largest company by May 31. This positioning solidified following Alphabet's April 29 Q1 2026 earnings beat, with revenue up 22% to $109.9 billion and Google Cloud surging 63% to $20 billion, triggering a historic $420 billion single-day market cap gain that widened its lead over Apple's $4.31 trillion by over $500 billion. NVIDIA's 9.3% odds reflect volatility risks ahead of its May 20 earnings report, where a potential 8-9% share drop could swap the top two spots. Apple's 2.5% hinges on closing the valuation gap amid sector rotation, while others like Microsoft and Broadcom lag distant benchmarks.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any active DOJ or FBI prosecution despite longstanding scrutiny over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein. Newly unredacted 2019 FBI files listed Wexner as a potential co-conspirator, prompting a February 2026 House Oversight Committee deposition where he denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes, claiming he was "duped" by his financial manager. Civil lawsuits, including one filed April 6 alleging Wexner funded Epstein's activities, continue, but no indictments have followed congressional inquiries or Attorney General questioning. With no recent grand jury activity or official announcements in the past 30 days, barriers to swift charges remain high absent major new evidence.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 23°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: A weakening polar air mass from the cold front that swept through São Paulo over the May 9-10 weekend—dropping highs to 16°C and lows to 12°C—has prompted forecasts of a temperature rebound on May 12, with Climatempo projecting a daytime high of 26°C under mostly sunny skies with morning clouds and evening fog, zero precipitation chance, and light easterly winds. This aligns with regional model consensus amid autumnal cooling trends, where May averages 23°C highs, occasionally varying by 3-5°C due to frontal passages. Official INMET monitoring and CGE updates expected Monday could adjust for any lingering instability, as measurement at key stations like IAG-USP determines resolution amid inherent short-term forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Will "Michael" be the April film with the highest domestic gross on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the title of the film released in April 2026 with the highest domestic gross between opening and May 30, 2026 according to "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page.

In the event of an exact tie the film that comes first alphabetically will be considered the winner.

If there is no final data available by June 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie commands 99.1% market-implied odds as the highest-grossing April 2026 domestic release, propelled by its record-shattering $131 million opening weekend on April 1 and sustained family audience legs that propelled it past $407 million domestically by early May, topping charts for at least three consecutive weekends amid glowing word-of-mouth and Nintendo's proven franchise draw from the 2023 hit. Competitors like the Michael Jackson biopic trail far behind despite a solid $97 million debut, hampered by mixed critical reception, while indies such as Mother Mary and Wasteland Cop posted negligible limited-release hauls under $200,000. Realistic upsets would require an unprecedented late-May surge from a challenger via viral social buzz or expanded theatrical runs, though Mario's multi-week dominance and minimal competition make such shifts improbable before the May 31 snapshot.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $296 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon shares hover near $272, close to the 52-week high of $278.56 touched on May 5, propelled by Q1 2026 earnings on April 29 that surpassed forecasts with $181.5 billion in revenue—up 17% year-over-year—and $2.78 earnings per share versus $1.65 expected, fueled by AWS's 28% growth to $37.6 billion and record 13.1% operating margins. Trader consensus prices in robust AI infrastructure spending and cloud reacceleration, offset by concerns over a $200 billion capex outlook; analysts' average 12-month target stands at $313, with highs to $370. Watch Q2 earnings on July 30, ongoing logistics expansions, and macroeconomic risk appetite through month-end for potential price threshold tests.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Victor Wembanyama: Rebounds O/U 12.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 10 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Victor Wembanyama records more than 12.5 total rebounds during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Victor Wembanyama records 12.5 rebounds or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Spurs vs. Timberwolves
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 10 at 7:30PM ET:
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the Timberwolves win, the market will resolve to "Timberwolves".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold a 2-1 series lead over the Minnesota Timberwolves entering Game 4 of the Western Conference semifinals at Target Center, driven by Victor Wembanyama's playoff dominance—including a 39-point, 15-rebound masterpiece in Friday's 115-108 Game 3 win—and a decisive 133-95 Game 2 rout that showcased their superior depth from a 62-20 regular season. Minnesota, the No. 6 seed at 49-33, stole Game 1 104-102 but has battled injuries, with Donte DiVincenzo out for the series (Achilles tendon repair); Anthony Edwards and Ayo Dosunmu are now fully available after left knee bone bruise and right heel/calf concerns, respectively, boosting their home-court edge. Spurs remain healthy, positioning trader consensus around their momentum amid Wolves' urgency to even the series.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 2 match between Tricked and MOUZ NXT in the NODWIN Clutch Series Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 10 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Tricked and MOUZ NXT play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Tricked vs MOUZ NXT (BO3) - NODWIN Clutch Series Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 2 match between Tricked and MOUZ NXT in the NODWIN Clutch Series Group Stage, initially scheduled for May 10 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Tricked" if Tricked win the match against MOUZ NXT.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ NXT" if MOUZ NXT win the match against Tricked.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tricked, ranked #44 on HLTV, enters the NODWIN Clutch Series #8 group stage BO3 against lower-ranked #139 MOUZ NXT after a 1-2 loss to AM in BC.Game Masters on May 7, though buoyed by four straight prior wins including against Walczaki and SINNERS. MOUZ NXT gained momentum with a dominant 2-0 victory over AM yesterday (Mirage 13-5, Nuke 13-11) in Swiss round 1, improving to 1-0 while advancing top teams via double-elimination. Tricked previously defeated MOUZ NXT 2-0 in February's PGL Bucharest qualifier amid their 6-1 map head-to-head edge overall, but both squads show mixed recent form (Tricked 47% win rate, MOUZ NXT 48% last three months) with stable rosters—Tricked's IceBerg, salazar, leakz trio vs. MOUZ NXT's opdust, ay0k, Nikodeon core—setting up a matchup hinging on map vetoes and clutch plays in the $50,000 B-Tier event.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats hold the largest bloc of 38 seats in the Folketing following the March 24 snap election, sustaining her 60% trader consensus as the most likely next leader despite her coalition talks stalling. King Frederik X's May 8 appointment of Venstre leader Troels Lund Poulsen as formateur—after Moderates' Lars Løkke Rasmussen blocked left-leaning deals—has elevated Poulsen to 15.3% and Rasmussen to 12.8%, reflecting center-right momentum amid fragmented proportional representation results with no majority bloc. Recent Verian polls show Social Democrats at 21%, but negotiations resuming May 11 could tip toward a centrist or blue-leaning coalition government.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD hovers near 1.178, buoyed by Eurozone April CPI accelerating to 3.0% year-over-year from 2.6% in March, signaling sticky inflation that tempers ECB rate cut expectations at its current 2.00% deposit facility rate. This contrasts with the US Federal Reserve's federal funds target of 3.5%-3.75%, where March CPI rose 3.3%, supporting a 150-basis-point policy differential favoring the dollar and capping euro upside. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects divided 2026 forecasts ranging from 1.08 to 1.28, driven by growth divergence and risk sentiment. Watch the Fed's June 16-17 FOMC meeting and ECB's July 22-23 decision for shifts in rate path expectations.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. markets.question: Games Total: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if LOS and Fluxo W7M play 4 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 4 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: LOS vs Fluxo W7M (BO5) - CBLOL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LOS" if LOS win the match against Fluxo W7M.

This market will resolve to "Fluxo W7M" if Fluxo W7M win the match against LOS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the CBLOL 2026 Split 1 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinals, 4th-seed LOS faces 5th-seed Fluxo W7M in a pivotal BO5, with the winner advancing to potentially face LOUD after their 3-1 upset over 3rd-seed Vivo Keyd Stars. LOS enters with strong momentum, boasting a perfect 5-0 head-to-head series record against Fluxo this split—including a 2-1 league stage victory on May 3—bolstered by their 4-3 regular season finish and even 9-9 game differential. Fluxo, also 4-3 but with a +2 game edge (9-7), seeks revenge after recent losses, highlighted by their upset over LOUD, in their first playoffs BO5 amid double-elimination format. No roster changes reported, emphasizing stylistic matchups and macro execution in current patch meta.

Home form and rivalry intensity could fuel an Anyang upset, but Jeonbuk's attacking depth via Lee Seung-woo positions them as consensus favorites.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid fragile ceasefire holding despite US strikes on Iranian tankers near the Strait of Hormuz and subsequent reprisals as of May 9, Washington awaits Tehran's response to a truce extension proposal relayed via Pakistani mediators, directly influencing trader consensus on attendees for the next US-Iran diplomatic meeting. The prior Islamabad round in early April collapsed after marathon talks, with US Vice President JD Vance, envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner facing Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, yielding no agreement on nuclear curbs or sanctions relief. President Trump signaled a potential deal "any day," while Iran's recent consultations with China add uncertainty; similar delegations likely unless escalation prompts higher-level involvement like Secretary Rubio or Supreme Leader proxies, with Oman or Pakistan as probable venues.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Central Weather Administration observations at Taipei Songshan Airport confirm the highest temperature on May 10 reached 29°C mid-afternoon, aligning with morning forecasts projecting northern Taiwan daytime highs of 26–29°C under persistent cloud cover and high humidity from an ongoing cool front. This follows yesterday's peak of just 22°C, reflecting subdued solar heating and stable atmospheric conditions typical of early Mei-Yu season influences, where frontal systems cap intensities. Trader consensus at 100% implied probability for 29°C reflects this verified data amid falling evening temperatures post-sunset around 6:28 p.m. Realistic challenges would require anomalous clear skies or downslope winds boosting late-day heating beyond model ensembles, unlikely per current NOAA and CWA guidance with final Wunderground records pending.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".
